plainEnglishInsights: Overall, ElectricNOW’s latest measured week shows that measured platform audience grew 68.6%, viewing grew 78.9%, and time spent softened by 16.9 minutes per active user. The short version is that we are separating real platform use from landing-page acquisition, so this read focuses first on whether people are using and watching ElectricNOW, then explains traffic sources, sales, and tracking caveats below.

Ad-performance audit for May 11-17, 2026 (review of internal email): Android active-user growth (+107.8% WoW) and iOS active-user growth (+61.6% WoW) are confirmed in GA4; the watched-video user count (~2.4K), the average-time-per-user decline of ~17 minutes, and the low video-completion rate (~10%) are also confirmed. However, total paid spend, total clicks, CPI, CPC and per-creative cost figures (Poly Couple, The Ark, UGC videos) come from Nathan's Google Ads / Meta accounts and cannot be independently verified from Electric's available data - GA4 only sees one linked Google Ads campaign (PCSM - ElectricNow App Promotion - Android: $105.55 spend, 821 clicks, $0.13 CPC, 466 conversions). The 2,864 reported installs is a paid-attributed subset, not total GA4 first_open events (which were 4,024). YTD purchase count (~27) also reflects Stripe/Apple/Roku reporting, not GA4 in_app_purchase events (7 YTD).
Measured ElectricNOW platform audience was 7.8K GA4 active users excluding landing-page acquisition paths, up 68.6% from the previous week.
Acquisition traffic is tracked separately: property-wide GA4 active users including landing-page traffic were 7.9K, up 69.6% from the previous week; landing-page users were up 54.2%.

Measured Apple/Android app-stream usage improved: 4.9K active users this week, up 217.5% from launch baseline and up 88.6% from the previous week; Apple TV is not yet split out from the Apple/iOS stream.
Viewing also improved: 2.1K app users watched on-demand or Live content, up 187.3% from launch baseline and up 78.9% from the previous week.
Roughly 42.7% of app users watched something, compared with 47.2% during launch week and 45.0% the prior week, so the viewing conversion rate is steady while the audience is growing.
On-demand is carrying most of the viewing growth with 2.1K starter users; Live remains meaningful at 40 viewers, down 13.0% from launch baseline and up 17.6% from the previous week.
Actual viewing behavior is measured separately: 2.5K users triggered a viewing event, up 64.0% from the previous week; video-complete users were up 45.3%.
Average time on platform is 41.8 engaged minutes per active user across all GA4-tracked platforms, up 16.7 minutes from launch week and down 16.9 minutes from the previous week.
Read this as two different measures: average time per user shows total weekly attention per person, while average time per session shows how long each individual visit or viewing session lasted.

Visible platform sales now combine Stripe, Roku, and the manual Apple App Store snapshot: 5 purchases and $31.95 in the latest 7 complete days, plus 0 purchases and $0.00 so far this week; this still excludes Google Play purchases. The Apple App Store contribution is currently a manual snapshot of $69.96 gross, or about $48.97 after Apple’s 30% platform fee.
Top watched programs in GA4 this week include ElectricNOW, The Ark, Leverage, Leverage: Redemption, and Almost Paradise.
The most watched Live channels generated 4.2K Live play events, led by ElectricNOW, ElectricNOW en Español, ToonOvation, Kung Fu Movies, and Daily Flash.

GA4, Stripe, Roku, and the manual Apple App Store snapshot should still be reconciled with Google Play, Amagi, ad-server, YouTube, Meta, and final app-store reporting before making final business decisions.
instrumentationGaps: No separate GA4 event found for go_ad_free_click yet.
No separate GA4 event found for video_purchase yet.
No separate GA4 event found for collection_purchase yet.

Stripe is now one visible sales source in the dashboard; total sales still require Apple App Store and Google Play reporting, and GA4 purchase events still need to be split into go-ad-free, individual video, and collection purchase actions.
Live TV should be reconciled with Amagi and ad-server reporting.

Google Ads account access is held by the vendor (Nathan); Electric's GA4 only sees one linked Google Ads campaign (PCSM - ElectricNow App Promotion - Android: $105.55 spend, 821 clicks, 466 conversions for May 11-17). Total paid spend, clicks, CPI/CPC, per-platform CPI, and per-creative cost/download figures in vendor emails are not independently verifiable from this property and should be labeled 'vendor-reported'.

Paid-install attribution is vendor-reported. GA4 recorded 4,024 first_open events for May 11-17 (Android 2,497, iOS 1,527); the 2,864 installs figure in the vendor email is a paid-attributed subset and should not be read as total app installs.

Per-title ad_complete share (e.g. The Ark vs Poly Couple) and per-creative cost/download require a title or creative dimension on the ad_complete event that GA4 does not currently expose; these comparisons rely on the ad server / Meta Ads Manager and are not reproducible from GA4 alone.

GA4 in_app_purchase coverage is incomplete: GA4 has logged 7 in_app_purchase events / 5 users / $134.93 revenue YTD against the vendor-reported ~27 purchases. Source-of-truth for purchases is Stripe + Apple App Store + Roku + Google Play, not GA4.

live.note: Live usage is based on GA4 screen name 'Live' plus playback events that also carried the Live screen name.

onDemand.note: On Demand usage is based on GA4 screen name 'On Demand'. Title-level viewing is shown below from GA4 video_title play events.

titleViewership.note: Top programs roll up fragmented GA4 play-event labels into parent shows/collections where possible, then use a GA4 filter to estimate deduped active users for each program. Play events remain the ranking metric because ads monetize playback; active users are total GA4 users who triggered matching play events for that program during the period. Treat this as GA-tracked viewership, not a fully cleaned catalog report yet, until the app sends content_type, series_title, season, episode, and video_id consistently.

metaCampaignContext.note: Meta campaign objective shifted from landing-page traffic, to app-store button-click leads, to direct app-install campaigns starting Apr 28. The app-install phase bypasses the website landing page, so lower landing-page traffic is expected and should not be judged as a standalone negative.
metaCampaignContext.appInstallBreakout: Through May 3, Meta reported 516 iOS installs at about $0.65 each and 949 Google Play installs at about $0.41 each.
metaCampaignContext.interpretation: Judge the newest phase by installs, app opens, viewing, Live usage, purchases, and ad value rather than by landing-page traffic.

launchBaseline.note: Platform time is based on GA4 userEngagementDuration across tracked ElectricNOW platform surfaces, excluding landing-page acquisition paths. Use this as the cleaner attention metric; GA4 average session duration is retained for context but may overstate streaming attention when sessions remain open.

previousWeek.note: Platform time is based on GA4 userEngagementDuration across tracked ElectricNOW platform surfaces, excluding landing-page acquisition paths. Use this as the cleaner attention metric; GA4 average session duration is retained for context but may overstate streaming attention when sessions remain open.

Use this as the cleaner attention metric; GA4 average session duration is retained for context but may overstate streaming attention when sessions remain open.

appSectionUsage.metric: GA4 active users and engaged time

rows.definition: Home/content browsing activity inferred from GA4 screen and path labels such as Home, On Demand, PDP, Player, Category, and Search.
Spanish-language section activity inferred from GA4 labels containing En Espanol, Español, Espanol, Spanish, or matching paths.
Live TV activity inferred from GA4 Live screen/path labels.
Saved or personal-list activity inferred from My Stuff, My List, Mylist, Watchlist, and Favorites labels.
Settings/account activity inferred from Settings, Account, Profile, and Preferences labels.

appSectionUsage.note: This section uses the current GA4 screen/path labels to infer the five app navigation areas in the order Home, En Espanol, Live TV, My Stuff, and Settings. A user can appear in more than one section. For cleaner reporting, the app should send a stable app_section value with exactly these five section names.

rokuAppEngagement.sourceDetail: Roku app engagement is parsed from the latest Roku App Engagement PDF emailed from bdp_noreply@data.roku.com. It measures Roku app/channel-store usage and is shown separately from GA4 app/web metrics.
rokuAppEngagement.reportPeriod: 8 days ago for 7 days Time Grain is day

visitSummary.source: GA4 sessions on tracked ElectricNOW platform surfaces, excluding landing-page acquisition paths.
visitSummary.note: Visits are GA4 sessions, not unique people. Weekly visits use the latest 7 complete days and compare against the previous 7 complete days. Landing-page traffic remains acquisition-only and is not included here.

manualAppleSalesUpdate.dedupeNote: Existing dashboard had one undated Apple Bundle 30 sale from Apr 6-May 10; new screenshots are dated May 13 and May 18, so these were treated as new non-overlapping sales.

adPerformanceAudit.label: Ad-performance audit: May 11-17, 2026
adPerformanceAudit.source: Internal email from Nathan reviewed against GA4 property 497892271
adPerformanceAudit.accessCaveat: Electric does not have direct access to the Google Ads account Nathan uses; GA4-linked Google Ads data shows only one Android Google Ads campaign slice. Total paid spend, clicks, CPI, CPC, per-platform CPI, and per-creative cost/download figures should be treated as vendor-reported and not independently verified.

confirmed.claim: Average time per user declined ~17 minutes WoW
confirmed.ga4Reading: Dashboard avg engaged minutes per user: 58.70 -> 41.82, delta -16.88 min

Dashboard videoCompleteUsers 263 / viewingEventUsers 2,504 = 10.5%. Direction and magnitude match (GA4 per-platform sum of unique video_complete users is 216, lower than the de-duped dashboard total of 263)

closeButImprecise.ga4Reading: Dashboard appActiveUsers = 4,912 (matches). GA4 simple platform sum is 5,115 because some users use both platforms.
Dashboard appEngagementRate = 82.37% (matches). GA4 weighted by sessions = 84.14%.
Dashboard viewingEventUsers = 2,504. GA4 simple sum of unique video_start/play users by platform = 2,400.

vendorReportedNotVerified.ga4Reading: Only $105.55 in linked Google Ads cost is visible in GA4 (PCSM - ElectricNow App Promotion - Android). Meta spend, full Google Ads spend, and any other platforms are not in this GA4 property.
GA4 shows 821 advertiserAdClicks at $0.13 CPC for the single linked Android Google Ads campaign. Other clicks/CPC come from Meta and other paid sources not joined to GA4 cost data.

vendorReportedNotVerified.claim: Total installs 2,864; Android 1,842 @ $0.62-$0.63 CPI; iOS 1,022 @ $1.08-$1.16 CPI
vendorReportedNotVerified.ga4Reading: GA4 first_open events May 11-17: Android 2,497 / iOS 1,527 / total 4,024 (application_install events: Android 2,473 / iOS 1,481 / total 3,954). The 2,864 reported installs is a paid-attributed subset rather than total app installs. Per-platform CPI relies on vendor spend Electric cannot see.
vendorReportedNotVerified.status: Vendor-reported and partially inconsistent with GA4 install events

vendorReportedNotVerified.claim: UGC-style video ads carry performance; Poly Couple has ~3x cost/download
vendorReportedNotVerified.ga4Reading: Creative-level cost/download is not present in GA4. No way to verify from Electric-side data.

vendorReportedNotVerified.claim: Poly Couple ~2% of ad_complete events; The Ark over 35%; ~7x volume difference
vendorReportedNotVerified.ga4Reading: GA4 app-stream ad_complete totals May 11-17: Android 7,233 events / 355 users, iOS 7,696 events / 312 users (total 14,929 events). GA4 does not currently carry a per-title / per-creative dimension on ad_complete, so the within-title split cannot be reproduced.

vendorReportedNotVerified.claim: Top content driving watch time: The Ark 6.8K play events, Leverage 5.5K, ElectricNOW Live ~10K events
vendorReportedNotVerified.ga4Reading: Dashboard already lists top watched titles (ElectricNOW, The Ark, Leverage, Leverage: Redemption, Almost Paradise) and top Live channels with 4.2K Live play events; exact per-title event counts were not re-pulled in this audit.

conflictsWithGa4.ga4Reading: GA4 in_app_purchase events Jan 1-May 17 2026: 7 events / 5 users / $134.93 revenue (Android 4 / iOS 3). Nathan's ~27 figure must come from Stripe + Apple App Store + Roku + Google Play reporting; GA4 is undercounting purchase events.
conflictsWithGa4.status: GA4 purchase tracking is incomplete; trust the storefront / Stripe rollup for revenue, not GA4 events

ga4LinkedGoogleAds.note: This is the only Google Ads campaign with cost/clicks visible in GA4 for this window. It does not represent the full paid buy.

topFirstUserSourcesAndroid.source: google / cpc (PCSM - ElectricNow App Promotion - Android)

ga4InstallEvents.note: Nathan's 2,864 installs figure does not equal total GA4 first_open events (4,024) and is likely the paid-attributed subset.

ga4ViewingAndCompletion.note: Video-completion problem direction is confirmed but exact user counts differ from Nathan because dashboard videoCompleteUsers (263) is GA4's de-duped cross-platform unique-user metric, while the per-platform simple sum is 216.

ga4PurchasesYearToDate.note: GA4 in_app_purchase coverage is incomplete; rely on Stripe + Apple + Roku + Google Play for purchase counts and revenue.

adPerformanceAudit.summary: Nathan's qualitative read on the week is largely correct: Android and iOS active-user growth, the average-time-per-user decline, and the weak video-completion rate are all confirmed in GA4. However, every dollar/click/CPI/CPC figure and every per-creative or per-title cost comparison is vendor-reported and cannot be independently verified from Electric's available data, because Electric does not have access to Nathan's Google Ads account and GA4 sees only a small slice of Google Ads cost. The total-installs figure (2,864) is a paid-attributed subset rather than total GA4 first_open events (4,024). The YTD purchase count uses storefront reporting, not GA4.
